explanations.1: IfModule checks if a specific Apache module is installed before running the code inside it, so your server won't crash if the module doesn't exist.
explanations.3: This header tells the browser to reuse the same connection for multiple requests instead of opening a new connection each time, which speeds things up.
explanations.11: The comment says 1 hour but the code says 1 month — this is a mistake in the comment, the actual expiration time is 1 month.
explanations.14: Line 14 is missing the word 'plus' — it should be 'access plus 1 month' to match the other lines and work correctly.
explanations.35: AddType tells Apache to recognize .cur files as a specific image type, so browsers know how to handle them.
explanations.56: This line uses seconds (2592000) instead of the time format used elsewhere, which is equivalent to 30 days but harder to read.
explanations.78: FilesMatch is a pattern that selects files based on their name — in this case, any file ending in .swf.
explanations.79: This sets an Expires header specifically for SWF files as a backup, in case the earlier ExpiresByType rules didn't apply.
